name: El Pantano del Bosquet — Circular walk from Moixent
name_original: El Pantano del Bosquet — Paseo circular desde Moixent
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Moixent traverse a diverse landscape at the foot of the Serra Grossa mountain range, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region is shaped by the Canyoles River, creating valleys with abundant vegetation, and features the expansive Valley of Les Alcusses, known for its vineyards, olive groves, and pine forests. Cyclists can explore routes with elevation changes in the mountains or gentler gradients along riparian areas. The area also includes the historic El Bosquet Reservoir, surrounded by pine and holm oak trees. Best touring cycling routes around Moixent The most popular touring cycling route is El Pantano del Bosquet — Circular walk from Moixent, a 13.9 miles (22.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 42 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the historic El Bosquet Reservoir, surrounded by pine trees and centenary holm oaks. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Public washhouse and Sants River – Riu dels Sants loop from Moixent, a difficult 32.8 miles (52.9 km) path. This route follows the Riu dels Sants, offering riparian scenery and a challenging ride. Local touring cyclists also love the Bar Restaurante Julio loop from Moixent, a 26.4 miles (42.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 37 minutes. Touring cycling around Moixent is defined by the Serra Grossa mountain range, the Canyoles River, and the agricultural landscapes of the Valley of Les Alcusses. The network offers options for moderate and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Moixent are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 25 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Moixent's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Moixent?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Moixent, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 7 moderate and 15 difficult options to explore the region's landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Moixent?
A: Moixent's touring cycling routes offer varied terrain. You'll find challenging climbs and scenic routes in the &lt;strong&gt;Serra Grossa mountain range&lt;/strong&gt;, gentler gradients along the &lt;strong&gt;Canyoles River&lt;/strong&gt;, and picturesque paths through the agricultural landscapes of the &lt;strong&gt;Valley of Les Alcusses&lt;/strong&gt;, often referred to as the 'Tuscany of Valencia'.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Moixent?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e919510784/el-pantano-del-bosquet-circular-walk-from-moixent" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pantano del Bosquet — Circular walk from Moixent&lt;/a&gt; takes you to the historic El Bosquet Reservoir. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14664232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Public washhouse and Sants River – Riu dels Sants loop from Moixent&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Riu dels Sants.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can visit while cycling in Moixent?
A: Moixent offers several scenic and historical points of interest. You can cycle to the historic &lt;strong&gt;El Bosquet Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt;, a charming spot surrounded by pine trees. Routes also offer views of the &lt;strong&gt;Serra Grossa mountain range&lt;/strong&gt; and the expansive &lt;strong&gt;Valley of Les Alcusses&lt;/strong&gt; with its vineyards and olive groves. For historical insights, consider routes that pass near the ruins of the &lt;strong&gt;Muslim Castle&lt;/strong&gt; or the &lt;strong&gt;Iberian Settlement of La Bastida de les Alcusses&lt;/strong&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Moixent?
A: While many routes in Moixent are moderate to difficult, the region's gentler sections along the Canyoles River and through the Valley of Les Alcusses can be suitable for families seeking less strenuous rides. Always check the route's difficulty and elevation profile before setting out with children.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Moixent?
A: The touring cycling routes in Moixent are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 25 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from mountain views to riparian scenery, and the well-maintained paths through agricultural areas like the Valley of Les Alcusses.

Q: Can I find routes that incorporate historical sites or cultural attractions?
A: Absolutely. Moixent is rich in history. You can find routes that lead to or pass by the &lt;strong&gt;Iberian Settlement of La Bastida de les Alcusses&lt;/strong&gt;, where the 'Guerrer de Moixent' was discovered, or the ruins of the &lt;strong&gt;Muslim Castle&lt;/strong&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31530526" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montesa Castle – Montesa Castle loop from Moixent&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that takes you past another significant historical landmark.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or features to see along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region boasts several natural highlights. The routes often traverse the &lt;strong&gt;Serra Grossa mountain range&lt;/strong&gt; and follow the &lt;strong&gt;Canyoles River&lt;/strong&gt;. You can also visit the historic &lt;strong&gt;El Bosquet Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt;, surrounded by pine and holm oak trees. For a refreshing stop, consider exploring routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3485518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Moixent?
A: Moixent's Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring, with lush landscapes in spring and comfortable conditions in autumn. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if cycling during this season.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the town of Moixent itself?
A: Yes, some routes provide excellent views of Moixent. The moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31524590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Cañoles – View of Moixent loop from Moixent&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to offer scenic vistas, including panoramas of the town and its surrounding landscapes.

Q: What kind of agricultural landscapes will I see on the touring routes?
A: Cycling through Moixent, especially in the &lt;strong&gt;Valley of Les Alcusses&lt;/strong&gt;, you'll experience a rich agricultural tapestry. The landscape is shaped by extensive vineyards, olive groves, almond trees, and cereal crops, interspersed with traditional farmhouses and pine forests, reflecting the region's 'Tuscany of Valencia' nickname.

Q: Are there opportunities for wine tourism integrated with cycling in Moixent?
A: Yes, Moixent is a significant winemaking region, and wine tourism is a growing aspect of its local economy. Many routes pass through the &lt;strong&gt;Valley of Les Alcusses&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its vineyards. Cyclists can often find opportunities to visit local wineries and enjoy tastings of the renowned local wines and olive oil, combining their ride with a cultural and gastronomic experience.
tips: Beautiful and quiet place just a few km from the town of Moixent. You can get here by paved road with very little traffic and take the short detour to the beautiful swamp. An ideal place to connect with nature and enjoy it surrounded by a wonderful environment.
